items.title:
RT @claudeai: Claude Security is now in public beta for Claude Enterprise customers.

Claude scans your codebase for vulnerabilities, valid…
items.snippet:
RT @claudeai: Claude Security is now in public beta for Claude Enterprise customers.

Claude scans your codebase for vulnerabilities, valid…

Internal/Other Changes
- Updated bundled Claude CLI to version 2.1.126

- Added origin to result messages (SDKResultSuccess / SDKResultError) — forwards the triggering message's SDKMessageOrigin so consumers can distinguish user-prompted results from task-notification followups

- The /model picker now lists models from your gateway's /v1/models endpoint when ANTHROPICBASEURL points at an Anthropic-compatible gateway
- - Added claude project purge [path] to delete all Claude Code state for a project (transcripts, tasks, file history, config entry) — supports --dry-run, -y/-

How do people seek guidance from Claude?

We looked at 1M conversations to understand what questions people ask, how Claude responds, and where it slips into sycophancy. We used what we found to impro
items.snippet:
How do people seek guidance from Claude?

We looked at 1M conversations to understand what questions people ask, how Claude responds, and where it slips into sycophancy. We used what we found to improve how we trained Opus 4.7 and Mythos Preview.
https://t.co/6tjY58uBhk

Here's how the winners combined multi-agent orchestr
items.snippet:
The Built with Opus 4.7 Claude Code hackathon is a wrap!  

Thank you to the 500 participants worldwide, and to @cerebral_valley for co-hosting.  

Here's how the winners combined multi-agent orchestration, persistent memory, MCP tools, sandboxed execution, and smart prompt design 🧵

items.title:
New on the Science Blog: We gave Claude 99 problems analyzing real biological data and compared its performance against an expert panel.

On 23 problems, the experts were stumped. Our most recent mode
items.snippet:
New on the Science Blog: We gave Claude 99 problems analyzing real biological data and compared its performance against an expert panel.

On 23 problems, the experts were stumped. Our most recent models solved roughly 30% of those—and most of the rest. https://t.co/BYqr76zxhk

items.title: Evaluating Claude’s bioinformatics research capabilities with BioMysteryBench
In new Anthropic Fellows research, we discuss “introspection adapters": a tool that allows language models to self-report behaviors they've learned during training—including potential misalignment.

items.title: Elevated errors on Claude Haiku 4.5
items.snippet: [minor] resolved — The incident is resolved. The elevated error rate was between 05:37 PT / 12:37 UTC and 06:58 PT / 13:37 UTC.

New Features
- Domain allowlist fields for sandbox network config: Added allowedDomains, deniedDomains, allowManagedDomainsOnly, and allowMachLookup fields to SandboxNetworkConfig, bringing parity with the TypeScript schema and enabling Python SDK users to configure network allowlists with proper ty
- Updated to parity with Claude Code v2.1.123
- Fixed OAuth authentication failing with a 401 retry loop when CLAUDECODEDISABLEEXPERIMENTALBETAS=1 is set

items.title: Replace transcript-style agent examples with prose trigger descriptions
items.snippet: Several agent files used <example> blocks containing user: "..." / assistant: "..." turn markers, embedded as \n-escaped strings inside the YAML frontmatter description: field. Replace those with flat prose trigger descriptions in description: and a 'When to invoke' section in the agent body contain

items.title:
AI has broken my mental model for time estimates. I used to lean on Pareto's 80/20: first 80% of the work takes 20% of the time. 

I think the underlying principle is still true, but both ratio and cu
items.snippet:
AI has broken my mental model for time estimates. I used to lean on Pareto's 80/20: first 80% of the work takes 20% of the time. 

I think the underlying principle is still true, but both ratio and curve have moved. Getting to "basically works" is nearly instant, sweating every single last detail st
